reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser zero-shot-object-detection pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls pipe(imageURL, [caption], {threshold:0.1, percentage:false}) — the phrases are joined into ONE period-separated caption (batch size 1, which the ONNX export requires) and grounded, returning [{label, score, box}]. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). The worker joins phrases into a single lowercased, period-terminated caption because passing an array batches the text input and the ONNX export rejects it ('input_ids … Got: N Expected: 1') — a real, documented constraint handled in code. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Runs once per prompt and filters the cached boxes via the slider.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = boxes grounding each caption phrase. Because one region can activate several phrase tokens, Grounding DINO returns a UNION label (e.g. 'a cat. remote'); the worker keeps that verbatim in rawLabel for the See-inside table and separately attributes each box to the best word-overlap input phrase for clean colouring/counts. Boxes drawn on the labelled canvas.
rubric.notes: The per-phrase label shown on boxes/counts is a heuristic (max word overlap with the input phrase), NOT the raw model label — the honest raw union is preserved in rawLabel in the See-inside surface. This is an honest, disclosed approximation of an ambiguous model output.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/grounding-dino-tiny-ONNX, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (zero-shot-object-detection), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~205 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'onnx-community/grounding-dino-tiny-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:205, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on onnx-community/grounding-dino-tiny-ONNX so the ~205 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Detection failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.

Real keyboard-operable controls: a caption/phrase text field, file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), Detect button and a Score-threshold slider with an <output> mirror.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — boxes, raw union labels, attributed phrases and scores all come from the real grounding pass. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

Result canvas has a descriptive aria-label; sample thumbnails have alt text; the phrase field is labelled; status is role=status aria-live; buttons have names. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The slider filters cached boxes with no re-inference. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology overlay boxes
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so assistive tech, translation and reading mode can reach it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — labelled canvas overlay + a See-inside table that shows BOTH the attributed phrase and the raw model union label (rawLabel), so the ambiguous output is legible.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + rawLabel column in See-inside

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — grounding runs in worker.js; slider filters cached boxes.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live, labelled phrase input, keyboard-operable dropzone/thumbnails, named buttons.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex-wrap layout + full-width canvas.

openQuestions: The per-box phrase attribution is a word-overlap heuristic over an inherently ambiguous union label — is there a cleaner, model-faithful way to map grounded tokens back to input phrases?
Automated scan flags sub-24px tap targets on both classes — needs manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Investigate mapping grounded token spans to input phrases via the tokenizer offsets instead of word overlap, to make box labels model-faithful.
Capture a retained headless run of a real caption grounding with measured latency so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for grounding-dino (onnx-community/grounding-dino-tiny-ONNX, zero-shot-object-detection, q8, ~205 MB, Apache-2.0). Real phrase-grounding run off-thread: phrases joined into one caption (a real ONNX batch-size-1 constraint handled in code), boxes drawn on a labelled canvas. Honest io-shape nuance flagged (io-shape 4/minor): box labels are a word-overlap heuristic over the model's ambiguous union label, with the raw union preserved as rawLabel in See-inside. No fresh measured latency captured here; sub-24px tap-target flags keep support at needs-review.
